checklist.entry: Use Evals to test model or application behaviour against defined criteria and data sources.
Keep eval data schemas, graders, run status and output-item review explicit.
Do not treat one-off manual examples as sufficient release evidence for high-risk model changes.
Design for rate limits, transient failures, retries, backoff and idempotency.
Do not claim current rate-limit values unless verified against the live project or official account settings.
Use Batch API only where asynchronous processing fits the user and system need.
Use moderation, adversarial testing and human review where the use case requires them.
Require human review for high-stakes outputs and code-generation outputs before practical use.
Make original evidence available to reviewers where outputs summarise, classify or interpret research data.
Record prompt-injection, data-exfiltration, over-reliance and hallucination mitigations.
